content.full_text:
/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 16, 1988

Donna R. Betancourt
Acting City Clerk
City of Santa Monica
1685 Main Street
Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295

RE: Your request for advice
Our File No. A-88-208

Dear Ms. Betancourt:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act of 1974.1/

QUESTION

May a late contribution report be sent by electronic mail?

CONCLUSION

Yes, assuming the filing officer who receives the
electronically sent late contribution report is willing to make
a hardcopy of the report and place it in the file of the
candidate or committee which sent it.

FACTS

As Acting City Clerk for the City of Santa Monica, you
received a late contribution report via electronic mail. The
report was received within 24 hours after the contribution was
received by the filer and contained all the required
information.

ANALYSIS

Section 84203(a) requires each candidate or committee that
makes or receives a late contribution, as defined in section
82036, to report the late contribution to each office with
which the candidate or committee is required to file its next
campaign statement pursuant to Section 84215.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Betancourt, Donna
Page Two

Section 84203(b) states that a late contribution must be reported by mailgram, telegram, guaranteed overnight mail through the United States Postal Service, or personal delivery. It must be reported within 24 hours of the time it is made in the case of the candidate or committee that makes the contribution and within 24 hours of the time it is received in the case of the recipient.

Although the law specifies the manner in which a late contribution report must be sent, the intent of Section 84203(b) is met by electronic mail. The fact that electronic mail is not widely used or recognized does not negate the fact that all the information required by Section 84203(a) is provided in a timely manner by electronic mail.

However, since this information must be made available to the general public, a hardcopy must be made immediately and placed in the filer's file. Therefore, the filing officer who receives the report must be willing to make a hardcopy.

If you have any additional questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

by:
Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

SANTA MONICA

CITY OF

CALIFORNIA

City Clerk                                                                                                               1685 Main Street
                                                                                                                             Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295
                                                                                                                             (213) 458-8211


May 27, 1988

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Gentlemen:

I would appreciate receiving a ruling regarding the legality of
filing a late contribution report by electronic mail.  The FPPC
direction reads, "Late contribution reports must be filed by
telegram, mailgram, guaranteed overnight mail or by personal
delivery  within  24  hours of making or receiving the
contribution."  Please advise whether a late contribution report
filed with the City Clerk via electronic mail fulfills this
filing obligation.

(Please note that I made this request for a ruling by telephone
on this date, and was advised that filing by electronic mail is
an acceptable form of "personal delivery," provided that a
hardcopy is made of the electronic memo and is placed in the
candidate's file.  I was asked by the FPPC to make the request
for a ruling by letter.)

Sincerely,

[signature]

DONNA R. BETANCOURT
Acting City Clerk

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 2, 1988


Donna R. Betancourt
Acting City Clerk
1685 Main Street
Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295

Re: 88-208

Dear Ms. Betancourt:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 2, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

DONNA R. BETANCOURT
